intent: Incorporate a warmup phase of gradient ascent steps on the forget set using a uniform-target KL divergence to push forget data predictions toward uniform, followed by fine-tuning on the retain set with combined cross-entropy and symmetric KL divergence distillation to original model predictions to improve forgetting while preserving retain/test accuracy.
rationale: Performing initial gradient ascent on the forget set with a uniform KL loss encourages the model to 'unlearn' the forget data by pushing outputs toward uncertainty, effectively erasing their learned signal. Following this with retain set fine-tuning using symmetric KL divergence distillation to the original model predictions helps preserve knowledge on retained data and maintains test accuracy. This alternating two-phase approach was inspired by the Fanchuan method but simplified to a single forget ascent phase to keep training efficient and avoid crashes seen in prior more complex alternations.

intent: Incorporate a few gradient ascent steps on the forget set with a uniform-target KL divergence loss followed by fine-tuning on the retain set using combined cross-entropy and symmetric KL distillation to the original model's predictions to enhance forgetting while preserving retain accuracy.
rationale: Gradient ascent on the forget set pushes the model predictions toward uniform, effectively unlearning these samples. Following this, fine-tuning on the retain set with symmetric KL divergence distillation to the original model preserves the learned knowledge and maintains retain/test accuracy. This approach combines effective forgetting with knowledge preservation, inspired by the successful Fanchuan method and prior accepted runs that used uniform-target KL ascent plus symmetric KL distillation.

intent: Add contrastive feature loss between forget and retain batch features during forget set gradient ascent to drive forget features away from retain features, combined with symmetric KL distillation and CE fine-tuning on retain set to better forget while preserving accuracy.
rationale: Building on the successful approach of alternating gradient ascent on the forget set with uniform-target KL loss followed by retain fine-tuning with symmetric KL distillation, this method adds a contrastive feature loss during the forget set ascent phase to explicitly push forget set features away from retain set features. This helps the model unlearn the forget data more effectively by separating their feature representations from those of retained data, reducing interference. The retain set fine-tuning with cross-entropy and symmetric KL distillation preserves accuracy and original knowledge. Inspired by the Fanchuan method's use of a contrastive loss and the prior best accepted method's uniform KL ascent, this combined approach should improve forgetting quality without sacrificing retain/test accuracy.
